# Transcrição: 47. Trait 46 - Great Salespeople hold themselves accountable.

- **Fonte:** 47. Trait 46 - Great Salespeople hold themselves accountable.
- **Duração:** 01:49
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 01:49]

Great sales people hold themselves accountable. They don't need anybody else managing them or holding them accountable or telling them what to do or pushing them. These are extremely disciplined people. I remember somebody once tried to manage me. I'm like dude I don't need somebody to manage me. I need somebody to lead me. Okay? You just lead me. Encourage me. Okay? You know, tell me how big and great I can be. That's what I need. I don't need you to push me, shove me, or keep stats on me. I'll keep them myself. I want to keep stats on myself. Why? Because I want to measure. I don't want to get all, oh, Grant, you're doing a good job. Talk myself into something I'm not. I want to stat everything. I want to hold myself accountable. Phone calls, mail out, contacts, presentations, closes. Did I even ask for the close? I want to stat everything out. You get it? How many calls did I make? How many new customers did I call on? How many referrals did I ask for? I want to stat it all out and see they're all growing. How many videos did I do? How many links did I do? How many tweets did I do? How many posts did I do? How much communication am I doing? How many meetings did I go to? How many church events, political events? I want to stat that data and then stat this. How am I doing on my weight? How am I doing on my workouts? How am I doing with time with my kids? Time with my wife, right? Don't settle, man. Collect date on any and every activity that is important to you. So you can be the guy in the room. You can be the guy or gal in the room when everybody else is like, oh my God, I don't have time for my kids but I got my trophy and you're like, dude, I got time for my kids, my wife, my family, my future, my the things I love to do on the weekends, my ski trips, my business, my customers. Dude, you got it all. Why? Because you're not worried. You got your statistics. You're measuring yourself. You're being held accountable by you. That's what the greats do and then they can improve themselves from the stats they create.
